events.description: Join us for Utah's longest running triathlon with an amazing venue with fantastic scenery at Rock Cliff Recreation Area at Jordanelle State Park.  Named by Triathlete Magazine in 2023 as on of America's favorite triathlons, this one you don't want to miss.

events.title: Heber Valley Main to Main
events.description: Join us for an exciting race day! The 10K race starts at 7 a.m. from Heber City Park, taking you through beautiful Midway streets and back. The 5K race begins at 7:15 a.m. at the same location.
events.location: Heber City, UT

events.title: Deer Creek Half Marathon
events.description: The Deer Creek Half Marathon starts and finishes at Soldier Hollow Campground. An amazing area home to some of Utah's most incredible trails. The majority of this race is run along the beautiful Deer Creek Reservoir. This trail run is very beginner friendly for anyone deciding they want to give trail running a try. However, the rolling hills will still present a challenge for even the experienced runners looking to push themselves!
events.location: Midway, UT

events.title: Wasatch Half Marathon and 10K
events.description: Come join us in the cool mountain scenery at The Wasatch Half Marathon and 10k! We are really to share this wonderful mostly trail run course to the race calendar. This event is one you don't want to miss. Starting and ending near the Soldier Hollow Nordic Center they’ll be no shortage of breathtaking scenery with the rewards of accomplishing something great!  No need for shuttles at this event as you’ll be able to park and the start/finish line.
events.location: Midway, UT

events.title: Learn American Mahjong with Mahjong Hive
events.description: Join us for a relaxed, beginner-friendly evening where we’ll demystify the beautiful game of Mahjong. Come for good company, easy learning, and great snacks. This class is for: Total Beginners: Never touched a tile? Perfect. We love starting from scratch. Social Seekers: Great for friends looking for a new "game night" staple or individuals looking to meet new people. Strategy Lovers: If you en...
events.location: The Dainty Pear Co., 152 W 100 N, Midway, UT 84049

events.title: Oil Painting with Holli Yoho
events.description: Paint your own lemon still life oil painting with step by step help. Ages 16 and up. Whether you’ve never picked up a paint brush, it’s been awhile, or you’re still getting comfortable painting this class is for you! All materials are supplied and you’ll go home with a 5x7 oil painting. Come fall in love with oil painting! Only 15 spots Available! *Due to material costs and teacher preparation,...
events.location: The Dainty Pear Co., 152 W 100 N, Midway, UT 84049

events.title: Cookie Decorating with Sunny Daniels
events.description: Join us for a sweet, beginner-friendly evening learning the basics of sugar cookie decorating. You will practice simple piping and flooding techniques while creating a set of five beautifully decorated cookies to take home. No prior cookie decorating experience is needed. All supplies will be provided, and you’ll leave with new skills, a gorgeous cookie set, and a little extra confidence for yo...
events.location: The Dainty Pear Co., 152 W 100 N, Midway, UT 84049

events.title: Rock-n-Roll Train
events.description: The Rock-n-Roll Train is a 90 minute round trip ride on the historic Heber Train featuring great rock-n-roll music, trivia, and train history hosted by Mark Nelson, Executive Director. A great date night! You'll be singing along to some of the greatest rock hits ever. This year we will have a 21-and-older car with beer and wine available. You are not allowed to bring alcohol on the train! And, when the train returns you can stick around for a live concert featuring The Flashback Brothers. There will be food and drink and lots of good times at the Depot after the train! The onboard Train Bar will be open during the concert. Alcohol must be consumed on the train.
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Improv Comedy with the Belvederes
events.description: Family-friendly improv comedy is guaranteed to have you laughing out loud. Come enjoy the hilarious performance by the talented comedic performers. Perfect for a date night, friend's night out, or bring your family.
events.location: Timpanogos Valley Theatre, 90 West 100 North, Heber City

events.title: Heber Market on Main
events.description: Over the past two decades, the Heber City Farmer’s Market has been a weekly, summertime event that has welcomed visitors and locals alike to gather together in downtown Heber City to enjoy local food, drink, shopping, and live music. SCHEDULE: 5:00 - 9:00 PM: Market with booths and vendors 6:30 - 8:30 PM: Live music and FREE to all Public parking surrounds the park block except for no parking on 100 West. Additional parking at the Public Safety Building. hebermarket.com/events
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Bluebird Café Concert Series
events.description: In our beautiful outdoor amphitheater, three talented artists share both their music and the compelling stories that inspired their songwriting journeys. Doors open at 7pm, performances begin at 8pm. Performances: June- 19, 20 (Sold Out), 26, 27 (Sold Out) July- 10, 11 (Sold Out), 17, 18 (Sold Out) August- 21 (Sold Out), 22 (Sold Out), 28, 29 (Sold Out) Bench Seating is sold out for every performance. Lawn Seating is still available select dates.
events.location: Sundance Resort, 8841 N. Alpine Loop Road Sundance, Utah 84604
events.address: 8841 N. Alpine Loop Road Sundance, Utah 84604

events.title: Midway Farmers Market
events.description: The market is held every Saturday from 10:00 am to 1:00 pm at the Midway Town Square from June through October. Fresh local produce and local artisans are onsite selling goods at the farmer's market in Midway. See you there!
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Freedom Train
events.description: Show your Independence Day spirit with a fun ride on the annual Freedom Train. We depart on time for these patriotic 90-minute round trips to Decker Bay—great music on board. Wear your red, white, and blue! Veterans and Active Duty Personnel ride FREE (in General Admission coaches). Call the ticket office for details at 435-654-5601.
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Ice Cream Social at the Market on Main
events.description: You're invited to join us on Thursday, June 11, from 5-9 PM at the Heber Market on Main for our annual Ice Cream Social! It's a sweet treat for everyone in the community. The Chamber will provide FREE ice cream bowls from Roonies Ice Cream. Enjoy delicious ice cream with various toppings, courtesy of our participating businesses. Mark your calendars and stop by to enjoy some delicious locally-made ice cream, enjoy all the delicious toppings you desire, and get to know your local business owners and organizations. We look forward to seeing you there!
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Mountain Valley Stampede Rodeo
events.description: One of the best 3-day PRCA rodeos in the state! The Mountain Valley Stampede Rodeo happens Thursday, Friday, and Saturday. This rodeo is great family fun with the best stock from Powder River, the best cowboys and cowgirls from across the Intermountain West, and specialty acts during the rodeo. Free concert on Thursday night following the rodeo. Come early and enjoy the Wasatch County Fair Days happening all week long. GET YOUR TICKETS IN ADVANCE. Tickets for the rodeo and the derby go on sale in early June. Please be aware of ticket scams and third-party resellers. Only tickets purchased through Wasatch County Parks & Rec will be accepted.
events.location: Wasatch County Outdoor Arena, 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032
events.address: 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Princess and Pirate Train
events.description: Calling all Princesses, Pirates, and friends for this epic train adventure! Your 90-minute roundtrip ride includes interactive fun onboard with princesses, pirates, and hosts who will have you singing and dancing along. Each passenger receives a Twisted Sugar cookie and a juice box. Wear your princess, or pirate costume and come and have some fun!
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wild West Days Trains
events.description: You won't want to miss this fun and family-friendly Old West Train event! The Wild West Days Trains includes a short, 45-minute round-trip train ride. On board, you'll be entertained by fun western sing-a-longs - and the Heber Valley Railroad Saloon Girls. And look out for the Old West - Heber Valley Train Gang, who might try and rob the train! At the depot, enjoy live music and the Old West Gunfight skit at the Depot pavilion. Bring the whole family and enjoy the experience.
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Summer Concerts at the Depot
events.description: Free outdoor concerts happening on select dates this summer at the Heber Valley Railroad Depot. This is the place to be for live outdoor music! Enjoy these complimentary shows. Seating provided on a first come, first served basis. Dancing is encouraged!!
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Main-to-Main 5K %26 10K Races
events.description: Kicking off the Red, White & Blue Festival, Heber Valley Chamber is hosting the fifth annual Main-to-Main 5K & 10K races, sponsored by Intermountain Health Heber Valley Hospital. This family-friendly run takes place on Saturday, July 4, 2026, at 7:00 am. Complete your registration in advance and at the link below. Same-day registration is not available.
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.description: We're gearing up for this year's Wasatch County Demolition Derby! To see the daily heat schedule, visit destructive-behavior.com/schedule. This event will sell out. GET YOUR TICKETS IN ADVANCE. Tickets for the rodeo and the derby go on sale in early June. Please be aware of ticket scams and third-party resellers. Only tickets purchased through Wasatch County Parks & Rec will be accepted.
events.location: Wasatch County Outdoor Arena, 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032
events.address: 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Midway Music on the Square
events.description: Midway's summer concert series is happening at Midway Town Square on Tuesday nights from 6 - 8 pm. Bring a blanket or lawn chair. There will be food trucks and great music for the whole family. Come and support local musicians. FREE to the public.
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Wasatch Coin %26 History Club Meeting
events.description: Wasatch Coin & History Club meets on the last Tuesday of each month (except December) at 6 PM in the Wasatch County Public Library, Heber City. FOR MORE INFO: Mark Avery @ 615-482-9747, averyancients.com popsavery1@gmail.com & Dr. Lee McKenzie - leefmck@gmail.com
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Free Yoga on the Lawn @ Heber City Park
events.description: Enjoy this peaceful, guided yoga practice in a beautiful natural setting in the heart of Heber City. Bring your own yoga mat, water, and anything to help you feel comfortable. All yoga sessions are weather dependent. Sessions are all free, but donations are welcome.
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Soulful Sundays @ Heber City Park
events.description: Join us for a serene summer evening as Soulful Sundays shares local musical talents. This enchanting hour of music will fill the Heber City Main Street Park with soothing sounds, peaceful harmonies, and community enrichment. Every Sunday will feature new artists. Bring a blanket, gather with friends and family, and experience the magic of live music in the heart of Heber City. Admission is free—all are welcome!
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Midway Fourth of July Celebration
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day with our small town July 4th celebration sponsored by Midway Boosters and Midway City. This family-friendly morning of festivities begins early. The evening features a stunning fireworks display. SCHEDULE: 6:00 am: Wake Up Canon Blast from Memorial Hill 7:00 am: Sunrise Service and Flag Raising Ceremony at Midway Town Square 8:00 - 10:00 am: Pancake Breakfast at Midway Town Square 10:00 am: Children's Patriotic Bike Parade Join the Red, White, and Blue Festival day-time festivities at Heber City Main Street Park, 250 S Main St, Heber 8:00 pm: Utah Symphony in Midway (FREE TICKETS HERE)
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Wasatch County Library Community Concert Series
events.description: Free Concert Series @ Wasatch County Library The Wasatch County Library invites you to an evening of live music. The concert is family-friendly, free to attend, and open to everyone—bring a friend and join us at the library amphitheater during the summer months or indoors during winter and inclement weather for a vibrant night of music and community!
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Dominick Antonelli @ Cafe Galleria
events.description: Live Summer Music Series May through September (weather permitting) Every day but Mondays from 5:30pm – 8:30pm Sundays 10am – 1:30pm Dominick has been playing guitar for 21 years and writing original music for 15+ of those years. He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health and is currently studying Music and Recording Arts Technology which is taking his creative possibilities to new depths and heights. He has performed at festivals in Thailand, Costa Rica, and the East Coast (US), as well as in yoga classes, local gigs, and workshops where he incorporates live music, meditation, group energy healing and writing into one experience. He loves performing live and sharing the gifts that continue to be cultivated. Read more about Dominick
events.location: Cafe Galleria, 101 West Main Street

events.title: Wine Tasting %26 Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leisure. You'll ride in one of our beautiful vintage lounge cars. Tickets are sold either by the half table for 2 people (may be shared with 2 other guests), a full table for 4, and a limited number of single seats. Wines on the menu: Decoy Brut Cuvee Kumusha Sauvignon Blanc Cest La Vie Rouge (Pinot Noir & Syrah Blend) Larchago Tempranillo - Rioja, Spain Graham's Six Grapes Ruby Port
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: American Soldier Outdoor Concert
events.description: A tribute to Toby Keith Live at Heber City's Main Stage. Join us for a multi-day celebration of independence, family, faith, and country. Check out our lineup and join us for this annual celebration.
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Midway America 250 Celebrations 4th of July
events.description: There’s nothing quite like music in the mountains on the 4th of July. 🇺🇸🎶 As part of Midway’s America250 celebration we welcome you to join with us for an unforgettable evening with the Utah Symphony. It will be an evening filled with music that celebrates America. 📅 Saturday, July 4, 2026 🕗 8:00 PM 📍 Midway Town Square 🎟 Free to attend Bring your blankets, chairs and your patriotic spirit-this is a night you won’t want to miss!
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Mountain Valley Special Needs Round Up Rodeo
events.description: Plan to volunteer or just join the fun for the Mountain Valley Stampede Round Up Rodeo, formerly known as the Special Needs Rodeo. Happening the Friday morning of the Wasatch County Fair Days, the Princess Contest starts at 9:00 am. The Rodeo kicks off right after the Princess Contest. All participants must sign up in advance and will have the opportunity to experience a series of modified rodeo events. (Participants will not be on horseback for any event.) This Special Needs Rodeo is a FREE event for all!
events.location: Wasatch County Rodeo Grounds, 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032
events.address: 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Board & Donor Speaker Series: Purposeful Volunteering: Find Your Fit, Build Impact
events.description: Discover how to align your passions with meaningful service in this engaging panel discussion—moderated by Suzy Berg Lyon , featuring panelists Karen Scheible , Scott Beck , and Amy Macuga —on purposeful volunteering. Whether you’re new to giving back or looking to deepen your impact, this session will explore how to find the right opportunities, stay motivated, and make a lasting difference rooted in real needs in your community. Join us for an inspiring conversation filled with practical insights, personal stories, and actionable guidance to help you volunteer with intention and impact.
events.location: Blair Education Center, Park City Hospital, 900 Round Valley Dr, Park City, UT

events.title: Pro Active Alliance Monthly Speaker Series
events.description: June 3 – Wasatch Immigration Project We are honored to welcome Maggie AbuHaider, co-founder of the Wasatch Immigration Project, as our featured speaker and presenter. After serving in judicial clerkships and as a Civil Assistant United States Attorney handling complex civil defense and immigration appeals, Maggie has dedicated her legal career to helping individuals navigate the challenges of the U.S. immigration system. In 2023, she co-founded the Wasatch Immigration Project to provide compassionate legal support for asylum seekers and immigrant youth. Maggie will share insights into today’s immigration landscape, the real-life impact on families and communities, and the important work being done locally through the Wasatch Immigration Project. The event will be held at the Kiln Coworking Space in Newpark, 5:30-7:30. We hope to see you there! Please RSVP to let us know you are coming....
events.location: Kiln Park City, 1090 Center Dr., Park City

events.title: Family Friendly: Wind Chimes in Clay
events.description: Create a one-of-a-kind ceramic wind chime for your home or garden. Using basic hand-building techniques, you’ll shape, texture, and decorate your clay pieces to form the chime’s body. We’ll guide you through the process, from forming the pieces to attaching the chimes for beautiful sound. You’ll learn creative glazing techniques to give your wind chime a vibrant, lasting finish. No prior experience is necessary—just bring your creativity! The price is per project, and we welcome kids under 12 when accompanied by an adult.
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Trails, Trash, & Tunes
events.description: Summit County’s Stormwater Coalition will celebrate National Trails Day (June 6) with the return of Trails, Trash and Tunes. Come and participate in a community cleanup and weed pull, enjoy live music, kids’ activities, and give back to the land in honor of National Trails Day. Free lunch for the first 50 participants! Attendees can also earn raffle tickets and win prizes by visiting the educational booths staffed by local partners and county employees. Date: Saturday, June 6, 2026 Time: 12:00 – 3:30 PM Location: Newpark Amphitheater, 1258 Center Dr, Park City RSVP FOR FREE HERE
events.location: Newpark Amphitheater, 1258 Center Dr, Park City, UT

events.title: Park Silly Sunday Market
events.description: An ecofriendly open air market, street festival and commUnity forum where neighbors and friends come together to celebrate our community of fun and funky Park Sillians. With live music, vendors, gourmet foods and a beer garden and tiki bar ~ this is the place to be on select Sundays this summer on Historic Main Street in Park City.
events.location: Historic Main Street, Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: Art Summer Camps 2026
events.description: Fill your summer with creativity at the Kimball Art Center! Explore the wide world of art in our week-long summer camps, June 8–August 14. From painting and poetry to mosaics and film, all camps are led by passionate instructors and carefully planned for each age group. Mini Artist – Ages 4-5 Kids – Ages 6-12 Tweens – Ages 10-14 Teens – Ages 13-18 See all camps and summer schedule at kimballartcenter.org/camps/
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Main Character Monday – Teen Event
events.description: Teens ages 13 to 16 are invited to Wasatch County Library’s new program, “Main Character Monday!” Held monthly with a new theme each time, this teen gathering is a chance to snack, chat, and connect with others who share similar interests. The first Main Character Monday takes place Monday, June 8, from 4 to 5 p.m., featuring a Manga and Anime theme. Whether you’re a longtime fan or just curious about the genre, come enjoy the vibe with fellow teens. To learn more about Main Character Monday and other teen events, visit Library.WasatchCounty.gov or email Brittnie at bhecht@wasatch.utah.gov . See you soon at Wasatch County Library!...
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Demonstration Garden Volunteer
events.description: Help bees, butterflies, and other pollinators by assisting with our demonstration garden in front of the EcoCenter! Volunteers will help with soil amendment, weeding, and pruning the pollinator garden in front of the EcoCenter to maintain it for the growing season. This opportunity is a great way to learn about native and waterwise plants that do well in Park City. Volunteers will receive instruction and guidance upon arrival from gardening professional Colie Belieu ( yogaearthdesign.com ). Dress for the weather and for gardening. We’ll provide the tools and supplies. Everyone is welcome, from beginners to Utah Master Gardeners! Can’t stay for the whole time? No problem! Join for as long as you are able....
events.location: Swaner Preserve and EcoCenter, 1258 Center Drive, Park City

events.title: Free Mountain Bike Event at Park City Bike and Demos
events.description: Join us for a community mountain bike ride through the trails of Park City. We’ll kick things off with a 90-minute group ride departing from Park City Bike & Demos. After the ride, stick around for pizza, drinks, and a raffle featuring prizes from Momentous, POC, and Park City Bike & Demo. Raffle prizes include: Two $200 Momentous nutrition packages POC Kortal helmet POC Devour sunglasses Park City Bike & Demos shop gear This event is free and open to all riders. Spots are limited, so be sure to reserve yours on Eventbrite.
events.location: Park City Bike & Demos, Park City, UT

events.title: Park Silly Sunday Market
events.description: An ecofriendly open air market, street festival and commUnity forum where neighbors and friends come together to celebrate our commUnity of fun and funky Park Sillians. events.title: Summit County Dems Fundraiser
events.description: Wednesday, June 24 6:00 to 8:00 PM The Red Banjo 322 Main Street, Park City Featuring Special Guest Brian King, Chair of the Utah Democratic Party We’re launching this fundraising campaign with a fun, informal evening featuring community conversation, food, and an opportunity to help build a stronger, more effective local political organization rooted in competence, integrity, and community-focused leadership. Tickets are $100 and include buffet pizza and a drink ticket. We also offer a no-questions-asked sliding scale because we believe civic participation should be accessible to everyone. Your support will directly fund voter outreach efforts here in Summit County at a time when outside political spending and coordinated campaigning are expected to play a larger role in local races. If you cannot attend, we hope you will still consider making a contribution in support of this work!...
events.location: Historic Main Street, Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: June Listening Session
events.description: Explore the intersecting languages of quilting and synthesizers. On June 24, artists Mary Toscano and Andrew Rease Shaw lead one of two Listening Sessions. Engage with their collaborative project, LFO, currently on view in (Re)sounding. Experience Toscano’s quilts and the corresponding compositions by Shaw. The artists will discuss their project while inviting reflections gained from an evening of close viewing and deep listening.
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Wasatch Back Art Festival – Heber
events.description: The Wasatch Back Art Festival is a growing community art event held in Utah’s scenic Heber Valley, showcasing a wide range of original work from local artists and makers. The festival brings together painters, photographers, sculptors, and craftspeople for a vibrant outdoor experience celebrating creativity and craftsmanship. Visitors can explore artist booths, meet creators, and enjoy a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere that highlights the unique culture and artistic talent of the Wasatch Back region. Heber Main Street Park (250 S. Main Street) July 24th 12pm – 6pm July 25th 10am – 6pn July 26th 10am – 6pm...
events.location: Heber Main Street Park, 250 S. Main Street, Heber, Utah

events.title: The Business Table
events.description: The Business Table is a monthly gathering hosted by Team Cheever Plumbing & HVAC intended to spark meaningful relationships among business owners in the Wasatch Back. This event is invite only. Please apply at the link below, and join us for great conversation and food at a local Park City restaurant! Sign up for our next event here!
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Dominick Antonelli at Cafe Galleria
events.description: Dominick Antonelli is on his first national tour and he will be wrapping up his last official tour date in Midway, UT at Cafe Galleria on Saturday August 8th. He will be playing an evening of original music from 530-830pm. Please come out for an evening of story telling, great food and beverages, original music and to celebrate Dominick’s final stop on his first national tour. We look forward to seeing you!
events.location: Cafe Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community gatherings, and a Native Fashion Show. Sponsors and partners are invited to help elevate Native voices, support Native artists and entrepreneurs, and align their brand with a meaningful cultural experience rooted in respect, representation, and celebration. Event Schedule Friday, July 10, 2026 * Native Market 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Sundial Pavilion * Hoop Dance Performances 11:00 AM–5:00 PM Forum Stage, Canyons Village Saturday, July 11, 2026 * Native Market 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Canyons Village * Hoop Dance Performances 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Forum Stage, Canyons Village * Native Fashion Show 4:00 PM–8:00 PM Kokopelli Grand Ballroom...
events.location: Canyons Village, 4000 Canyons Resort Dr, Park City
